reasoning: Currency exchange rates typically follow a random walk (a martingale process) in the short to medium term. Therefore, the probability of the exchange rate of USD to GBP being strictly higher on a future date compared to its value on 2025-11-09 is approximately 50%. The current interest rate differential between the US Federal Reserve and the Bank of England is very small, meaning any drift due to covered interest parity is negligible over a horizon of a few months. Given this lack of strong directional drift, the best estimate for whether the exchange rate will increase by the unlisted resolution date(s) is a coin toss.
